thread: Can the seam in Frostmourne’s field be turned into a usable weakness?

evidence.clue: Jaina hears a repeatable crooked turn or seam in Frostmourne’s deflection field during the cave fight.
The seam is not yet usable in combat, but Jaina keeps working it physically and mentally during the retreat, treating it as the one thing worth carrying out of the loss.
On the march south, Jaina turns the seam from a felt hitch into a three-school framework: frost to force the turn, arcane to hold the seam open, then fire through the exposed gap.
At Capital City the framework gains a live bodily condition as well: Sylvanas must buy Jaina three to five still seconds at sword range for the sequence to work at all.
Jaina successfully reproduces the three-school sequence in the Imperial Chamber, forcing Frostmourne’s crooked turn into an open seam under combat pressure.
Once the seam is held open, ordinary attack geometry matters again and Sylvanas’s final arrow can kill Arthas.
The weakness is therefore real and usable, but exploiting it does not neutralize Frostmourne itself.

readerKnows: The seam was not a false hope. It can be forced, held, and exploited in a real fight. The blade can be wounded at the bond level even if that wound is not equivalent to full neutralization.
povCharacterKnows: Jaina and Sylvanas know the seam method works. They also know the method solves only part of the larger Frostmourne problem, because the sword remains active after Arthas dies.
notes: Resolved in chapter23 as a technical question. The remaining thread is no longer whether a weakness exists, but how to contain or end a damaged yet still-active Frostmourne.
